conciseness.ex: I admired the conciseness of his report.

concisely.ex: The rules were stated concisely tonight.

conclave.def: 秘密會議、紅衣主教團的人物 (單數) (密)
conclave.ex: The papal conclave met to elect a new Pope.

conclude.def: 結束、斷定、締結的人的人
conclude.ex: The jury will likely conclude he is innocent.

concluded.def: 結束了的、斷定了的人的人 (或物)
concluded.ex: The concluded agreement was signed today.

conclusion.def: 結論、推論、結束、締結的人物 (單數)
conclusion.col: reach a conclusion, jump to conclusion, in conclusion
conclusion.ex: In conclusion, we must act very quickly now.

conclusive.def: 決定性的、確鑿的的人的人物 (定)
conclusive.ex: The police found conclusive evidence of guilt.

conclusively.ex: The theory was proved conclusively today.

concoct.def: 調製、捏造、編造的人的人
concoct.col: concoct a plan, concoct a story
concoct.ex: They managed to concoct a plan for escape.

concoction.def: 調製品、捏造的人物 (單數)
concoction.ex: The chef served a very strange concoction.

concomitant.def: 伴隨的、伴隨物的人物 (單數)
concomitant.ex: Success is a concomitant result of hard work.

concord.def: 和諧、一致、公約的人的人的人物 (單數)
concord.ex: The two nations now live in perfect concord.

concordance.ex: The scholar compiled an index concordance.

concordant.def: 和諧的人、一致的人的人物
concordant.ex: Their views were concordant on most issues.

concourse.def: 中央大廳、群集的人的人物 (單數)
concourse.ex: The airport concourse was full of travelers.

concrete.def: 混凝土、具體的、實體的的人物 (實)
concrete.ex: Please give me a clear and concrete example.

concretely.ex: The plan was explained concretely today.

concretion.def: 凝結物、實體的人物 (單數)
concretion.ex: A hard concretion formed inside the pipe.

concubinage.ex: Concubinage was once common in this land.

concubine.ex: The king had many royal concubines at court.

concupiscence.ex: The priest warned against fleshly concupiscence.

concur.def: 同意、同時發生、互助的人的人
concur.ex: I concur with your expert opinion tonight.

concurrence.def: 同意、合流、併發的人物 (單數)
concurrence.ex: We need written concurrence from the board.

concurrent.def: 同時發生的、合作的人的人物
concurrent.ex: There were several concurrent local events.

concurrently.ex: The two programs are running concurrently.

concuss.ex: The fall managed to concuss the young player.

concussion.ex: He suffered a mild concussion in the game.

condemn.def: 譴責、判刑、宣告不適用的的人的人
condemn.ex: World leaders condemn the recent violence.

condemnation.ex: The act met with strong global condemnation.

condemnatory.def: 譴責的人、定罪的人的人物
condemnatory.ex: His report was written in a condemnatory tone.

condemned.def: 被判刑的、不安全的人的人 (或物)
condemned.ex: They lived in a small condemned building.

condensability.ex: The gas has high condensability tonight.

condensable.ex: Steam is easily condensable into water now.

condensation.ex: Window condensation made it hard to see out.

condense.def: 濃縮、縮短、冷凝的人的人
condense.ex: Try to condense the text into one page now.

condensed.def: 濃縮了的、簡略的人的人 (或物)
condensed.ex: She used condensed milk for the new cake.

condenser.def: 冷凝器、電容器人物 (單數)
condenser.ex: The optical condenser focused the light rays.

condescend.ex: She wouldn't condescend to speak to them.

condescending.ex: He has a very condescending manner today.

condescendingly.ex: The clerk was treated condescendingly today.

condescension.ex: He spoke with an air of condescension tonight.
